title: Login redirect loop caused by email claim mismatch after Okta-to-Entra ID migration
problem: After migrating SSO from Okta to Microsoft Entra ID, approximately 30% of users are stuck in an infinite authentication redirect loop. Users authenticate successfully with Entra ID but TaskFlow fails to establish a session, redirecting them back to the IdP repeatedly.
root_cause_hypothesis: TaskFlow matches authenticated users by comparing the email claim from the IdP against stored user records. Two populations are affected: (1) Users with '+' in their email addresses — the plus sign is likely being corrupted during the OAuth/SAML callback URL processing ('+' decoded as a space, or double-encoded as '%2B'), causing the email lookup to fail. (2) Users whose primary email changed between Okta and Entra ID — the email claim returned by Entra ID doesn't match the email stored in TaskFlow's user table from the Okta era. In both cases, the failed lookup means no session is created, and the auth middleware redirects back to login, producing the loop.
reproduction_steps: 1. Set up a user account in TaskFlow with a plus-addressed email (e.g., jane+taskflow@company.com)
2. Configure SSO with Microsoft Entra ID
3. Attempt to log in with that user
4. Observe: user authenticates successfully at Microsoft but gets redirected back to TaskFlow login in a loop
5. Alternatively: set up a user whose Entra ID primary email differs from their stored TaskFlow email (e.g., old Okta email was jdoe@company.com, Entra ID returns jane.doe@company.com)
environment: TaskFlow with Microsoft Entra ID SSO (recently migrated from Okta). Affects all browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox). Incognito windows occasionally bypass the issue, suggesting stale session cookies from Okta may be a contributing factor.
impact: ~30% of the team cannot log into TaskFlow at all. This is a complete blocker for those users with no reliable workaround.
recommended_fix: Investigate two areas: (1) In the SSO callback handler, check how the email claim is extracted and decoded — ensure '+' characters survive URL decoding intact (check for application/x-www-form-urlencoded vs proper handling of the '+' character in query parameters or POST bodies). (2) In the user lookup logic, consider matching on a stable identifier beyond just email (e.g., the IdP subject/oid claim), or implement a case-insensitive email lookup that also checks known aliases. For the migration specifically, either update stored emails to match Entra ID primary addresses, or add a mapping table for email aliases. Additionally, clear or invalidate any residual Okta session cookies that may be interfering.
proposed_test_case: Create test users with plus-addressed emails (user+tag@domain.com) and with mismatched primary emails between IdP claim and stored record. Verify that the SSO callback correctly resolves both to valid user sessions without redirect loops. Also verify that the '+' character is preserved through the full callback URL decode path.
information_gaps: Which protocol is used — SAML 2.0 or OIDC/OAuth 2.0 — would clarify exactly where the encoding issue occurs
Whether TaskFlow stores the IdP subject identifier (sub/oid) in addition to email, which would enable a more robust fix
The exact non-plus-addressed users affected beyond the email-change cases — there may be a third subpopulation
